note: Explorer no longer shows 'node offline' when getinfo 504s. Root cause of the recent dead-explorer reports: when the node is busy serving the miner over the same RPC, the heavy getinfo call (mempool+peers+cASERT+fees) can exceed nginx's proxy_read_timeout (~10s) and return 504 Gateway Timeout. The previous loadStats would then fall through to the offline banner and skip every other loader, so the page rendered nothing — even though the node was responding fine to lighter calls. New behaviour: when getinfo fails, loadStats falls back to getblockcount + getrawmempool and renders a DEGRADED view (latest block height + mempool size + a 'degraded: getinfo timed out (504)' note in the stats panel). loadBlocks / loadLatestTxs / loadMempool then run normally and populate the rest of the page. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
